title: Pellaea allosuroides
intro: Pellaea allosuroides, also known as the New Zealand button fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, button-like fronds. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Pellaea allosuroides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pellaea allosuroides is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Pellaea allosuroides is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand. This species is classified in the genus Pellaea, which is a group of terrestrial ferns.
